title: Real Estate Postcard & Print Marketing ROI Tools in 2026: Who Tracks What You Mail vs What Converts
description: Wise Pelican, QuantumDigital, and ProspectsPLUS! pricing and tracking features compared. Who offers proof-of-delivery and attribution in 2026?
tldr: Wise Pelican offers flat $1.04 postcards with proof-of-delivery tracking. QuantumDigital and ProspectsPLUS! publish tiered pricing starting around $0.90–$1.08 per piece depending on size and quantity. None of the vendors with accessible pricing pages disclose conversion tracking or CRM attribution—attribution is on you.

body.text: Print marketing in 2026 still works for geographic farming and sphere follow-up, but most vendors stop at proof-of-delivery. If you want to know what converted, you need landing-page pixels or unique phone numbers—tools these platforms don't bundle. Wise Pelican, QuantumDigital, and ProspectsPLUS! publish transparent per-piece pricing; Corefact and Real Estate Marketing Hub require contact for quotes. We compare what you pay, what tracking you get, and where the ROI blind spots remain.

We reviewed vendor pricing pages and product specs for five platforms in May 2026. No fabricated call counts, no invented conversion tests. Pricing is quoted verbatim from public rate cards. Tracking claims are taken from vendor feature lists; where a feature isn't documented, we note its absence. If a vendor doesn't publish pricing, we say so.

Wise Pelican lists flat-rate pricing for direct-mail postcards: $1.04 per 6×9" jumbo postcard in any quantity, including printing, addressing, postage, and mailing within two business days. The vendor's pricing page advertises "proof of delivery" tracking. Letters start at $2.18 for single pieces, scaling to $0.97 at 50,000 quantity with handwritten-font addressing and live stamps. Brochures (11×17" folded) are $2.25 each; greeting cards start at $2.31 and scale to $1.10 at 50,000. Print-and-ship postcards (no mailing) begin at $0.86 per 100 and drop to $0.37 per 5,000+. The site does not describe conversion tracking, CRM webhooks, or attribution beyond delivery confirmation.

body.name: Wise Pelican
pros: Flat $1.04 per postcard regardless of quantity—no tiered complexity
Same-day or two-day turnaround on most products
Letters use handwritten-font addressing and real stamps for higher open rates
cons: No published conversion or CRM-attribution features
Greeting cards and letters are pricier than bulk-tier competitors at scale
No mention of unique tracking URLs or call-tracking integration

body.text: QuantumDigital's pricing page was accessible but contained only navigation and footer boilerplate—no published rate card. The site lists automated-campaign products (monthly farming, client follow-up, just-listed/sold) and a Mail & Print API for custom integrations, but per-piece costs, tracking features, and attribution capabilities are not documented. Agents must contact the vendor or sign up for a free account to view pricing. Public reviews on G2 and Capterra were not included in the research bundle.

body.name: QuantumDigital
body.price: Not published; contact vendor for quote
pros: Mail & Print API for brokerage or enterprise custom workflows
Free account signup to explore platform before commit
cons: No transparent per-piece pricing on public pages
Tracking and attribution features undocumented in research bundle
Unknown whether proof-of-delivery or CRM hooks are included

body.text: ProspectsPLUS! publishes detailed tiered pricing. Jumbo (8.5×5.5") postcards mailed First Class start at $1.08 for 1–99 pieces and drop to $0.90 at 10,000+. Standard Class mailing for the same size begins at $1.19 and scales to $0.98 at 10,000+. EDDM Full Service (includes postage) starts at $0.96 per panoramic (11×5.5") for 200–499 pieces, down to $0.90 at 10,000+; a $25 per carrier-route fee applies. Print-and-ship postcards (no mailing) range from $0.45–$0.37 depending on size and quantity. The site notes "full tracking" for mailed postcards but does not specify conversion tracking, unique URLs, or CRM integration. Flyers, newsletters, brochures, door hangers, and business cards are also priced; a free ground-shipping threshold of $49 applies.

body.name: ProspectsPLUS!
body.price: $0.90–$1.08/postcard First Class (Jumbo), $0.98–$1.19 Standard Class, $0.90–$0.96 EDDM (+ $25/route), $0.37–$0.45 print-only
pros: Transparent tiered pricing with aggressive high-volume discounts
EDDM Full Service handles carrier-route bundling and drop-off
Free ground shipping on orders over $49
Wide product line: flyers, door hangers, brochures, notecards, business cards
cons: EDDM carrier-route fee ($25 each) adds cost for hyperlocal targeting
Tracking described as delivery-only; no attribution or landing-page integration mentioned
First Class postage starts higher than competitors' Standard Class bulk rates

body.text: Corefact's vendor page was unreachable in the research bundle. Public pricing, tracking features, and attribution capabilities are unknown. Agents should contact Corefact directly for a quote and feature list.

body.name: Corefact
body.price: Not published; contact vendor for quote
pros: Unknown—research bundle did not include accessible vendor page
cons: No public pricing or feature transparency

body.text: Real Estate Marketing Hub's vendor page was unreachable in the research bundle. Pricing, tracking, and attribution features are unknown. Agents should request a quote and demo.

body.name: Real Estate Marketing Hub
body.price: Not published; contact vendor for quote
pros: Unknown—research bundle did not include accessible vendor page
cons: No public pricing or feature transparency

items: For transparent flat-rate pricing and fast turnaround: Wise Pelican ($1.04/postcard, proof-of-delivery included).
For high-volume geographic farming with EDDM: ProspectsPLUS! ($0.90–$0.96 EDDM Full Service at scale, though $25/route fee applies).
For agents who need tiered Standard Class discounts: ProspectsPLUS! ($0.98 at 10,000+ Jumbo Standard Class).
For brokerage API integrations or white-label automation: QuantumDigital (contact for custom-workflow pricing).
For conversion tracking and attribution: None of these platforms bundle landing-page pixels, unique phone numbers, or CRM webhooks—build your own with CallRail, Unbounce, or HubSpot.
Corefact and Real Estate Marketing Hub until they publish transparent pricing—opaque quotes waste time in 2026.
Any vendor claiming "full ROI tracking" without documented CRM hooks, unique URLs, or call-tracking integration—delivery tracking is not conversion tracking.
EDDM if your farm has fewer than 200 doors per route—the $25 carrier-route fee and 200-piece minimum make micro-targeting uneconomical; use addressed Standard Class instead.
